2606.24595.title: MEMPROBE: Probing Long-Term Agent Memory via Hidden User-State Recovery
2606.24595.paper_summary: Long-term memory promises LLM agents that grow more capable across sessions, maintaining an accurate, evolving understanding of the user that interaction forms. In practice, however, this memory is evaluated mostly through downstream behavior, such as later answers, personalization quality, or task success, which tests that understanding only indirectly and leaves the memory artifact itself largely unaudited. We argue that long-term memory should instead be evaluated as an auditable post-interaction artifact: after ordinary assistance, what structured user state can be reconstructed from the memory the agent leaves behind? We instantiate this view in MEMPROBE, a benchmark in which a memory-equipped agent assists simulated users, each carrying a hidden, taxonomy-anchored user-state bank, across a trajectory of leak-controlled tasks, after which that bank is reconstructed from the agent's resulting memory under both full-store and top-k access. Built on synthetic ground truth for efficient, scalable measurement, MEMPROBE spans 50 simulated users with 31 hidden dimensions each (1,550 recovery targets) and tests 5 representative memory systems. Testing state-of-the-art memory agents, we find that successful assistance and recoverable memory behave as distinct capabilities. Task completion nearly saturates, even for a memoryless baseline, while category-balanced recovery stays moderate (about 0.6) and drops further under top-k retrieval. MEMPROBE is the first benchmark to study memory recovery directly, reconstructing the user state a system retains and scoring it against ground truth. We see recovery as a concrete objective for future memory agents to optimize, and MEMPROBE as a step toward an environment where agents are trained to remember their users, growing more faithful the longer they know them.
2606.24595.paper_summary_zh: 長期記憶承諾 LLM 代理人隨著會話的增長而變得更有能力，維持對用戶的準確、持續演變的理解，這種理解是通過互動形成的。然而，在實踐中，這種記憶主要通過下游行為進行評估，例如後來的回答、個性化質量或任務成功率，這些只間接測試了該理解，並且使記憶的實體本身在很大程度上未經審核。我們主張，長期記憶應該作為可審核的後互動實體來評估：在普通協助之後，從代理人留下的記憶中可以重建什麼結構化的用戶狀態？我們在 MEMPROBE 中具體化這一觀點，這是一個基準，其中一個配備記憶的代理人協助模擬用戶，每個用戶都攜帶一個隱藏的、以分類法為基礎的用戶狀態庫，並在一系列控制洩漏的任務中進行，之後該庫將根據代理人所留下的記憶在全存儲和 top-k 訪問下進行重建。基於合成的真實數據以實現高效、可擴展的測量，MEMPROBE 涉及 50 名模擬用戶，每名用戶擁有 31 個隱藏維度（1,550 個恢復目標），並測試 5 種具有代表性的記憶系統。在測試最先進的記憶代理人時，我們發現成功的協助和可恢復的記憶表現出不同的能力。任務完成率幾乎達到飽和，即使對於無記憶的基準，類別平衡的恢復保持在中等水平（約 0.6），並在 top-k 檢索下進一步下降。MEMPROBE 是第一個直接研究記憶恢復的基準，重建系統保留的用戶狀態並與真實數據進行比較。我們將恢復視為未來記憶代理人優化的具體目標，並將 MEMPROBE 視為邁向一個代理人被訓練去記住其用戶的環境的步驟，隨著認識時間的增長而變得更加忠實。
2606.24595.author: Enze Ma et.al.
2606.24595.authors: Enze Ma, Yufan Zhou, Wei-Chieh Huang, Jie Yang, Huanhuan Ma, Zixuan Wang, Chengze Li, Chunyu Miao, Philip S. Yu, Zhen Wang
